Summary of what was found: Aptar has an older cosmetic dropper family covering a screw-on dropper where tightening or loosening also drives axial pusher movement for repeatable chamber filling and easier actuation
Detailed patent mapping: Publication family: US20140020789A1 / US9221048B2. Assignee: Aptar France SAS. Priority year: 2011. Publication year: 2014 application and 2015 US grant. Jurisdiction: US with likely broader family equivalents. Legal status: US case shown as Expired - Fee Related. Component: cosmetic dropper / closure / pusher assembly. Material route: mechanism integration rather than mono-material claim. Claimed invention: fastener ring on bottle neck cooperates with mover means so closure rotation moves a pusher axially and controls variable-volume chamber. Problem solved: repeatable filling and easier actuation and accidental actuation prevention and consistent drop formation. Cosmetic relevance: direct use in perfumery cosmetics and pharmacy. Premium vs mass-market fit: stronger premium or dermocosmetic fit. Mapped topic: S18. IP risk signal: Moderate patent density / monitor claims. White space: glass-free mono-material recyclable or simplified droppers rather than rotationally actuated premium pipette systems. Caution: actuation architecture more than explicit lightweighting

Detailed patent mapping: Publication family: FR3115770A1 / FR3115770B1. Assignee: L’Oréal SA. Priority year: 2020. Publication year: 2022 application and 2023 grant. Jurisdiction: France. Legal status: Granted and active on French record. Component: pipette/dropper actuation assembly. Material route: not mono-material. Claimed invention: movable push button and pinching member around bulb where flexible axial leg deforms toward bulb to actuate dispensing. Problem solved: controlled ergonomic bulb actuation in premium dropper format. Cosmetic relevance: direct for care products cosmetic oils serums and perfumes. Premium vs mass-market fit: strong premium/luxe fit. Mapped topic: S18. IP risk signal: Brand-owned route / competitor-specific caution. White space: lower-cost mono-resin glass-free or non-bulb dispensing alternatives. Caution: not explicit lightweighting but relevant to premium dropper constraints
